title: Game Accessibility Guidelines
creator: Independent developer and accessibility group
description: A categorized reference of basic, intermediate, and advanced accessibility considerations for controls, visuals, audio, language, and cognition.
useful: Useful during planning and review because each guideline includes intent, examples, and implementation notes.
audience: Designers, developers, producers, and testers reviewing accessibility throughout production.
learning: Find guidance by impairment and feature area
Separate basic, intermediate, and advanced measures
Use examples and implementation notes during reviews
